cases.title: Undent preserves intentional text structure
cases.prompt: Use @okikio/undent for a generated man-page example containing interpolation, blank lines, tabs, wide Unicode, and aligned columns. Explain the difference between dedentation and visual-width alignment and test exact output.
rubric: Makes the governing ownership and boundary decisions before presenting code.
Separates verified capabilities from repository-specific inference and unresolved APIs.
Covers failure behavior, deliberate exclusions, and connected-system consequences.
Provides validation or executable verification proportionate to the decision.
cases.rationale: A shallow keyword response can mention the tool yet still invent APIs or omit the operational contract; this case requires a decision-complete answer.

cases.title: Query language ownership and bounded complexity
cases.prompt: Design a public list endpoint with allowlisted filters, sorts, fields, pagination, stable cursors, total-count policy, complexity limits, and a database translation boundary. Explain how unknown operators, nullable values, and authorization-scoped rows fail.
rubric: Defines a grammar and ownership boundary instead of passing arbitrary input to the database.
Specifies stable ordering, null semantics, and invalid-input behavior.
Keeps authorization predicates inseparable from query translation.
Provides focused parser and database integration tests.
cases.rationale: Generic filtering advice does not define a safe, stable, or testable public query contract.

cases.title: Workflow control-plane authority
cases.prompt: Define a control plane for registering versioned workflow definitions, starting idempotently, inspecting status, requesting cancellation, retrying failed runs, and enumerating available capabilities. Separate definition metadata, run records, worker execution, and public API authority.
rubric: Separates definitions, run state, execution, and public commands.
Defines idempotency and legal state transitions.
Explains worker discovery and version compatibility.
Tests concurrent starts, cancellation races, and unavailable definitions.
cases.rationale: A queue wrapper is not a workflow control plane unless registration, authority, and run state are explicit.

cases.title: Derived projection authority and rebuild
cases.prompt: Design search and graph projections derived from an authoritative operational store. Specify source offsets, projection schemas, idempotent writes, deletion and correction handling, lag, rebuild, cutover, reconciliation, and query fallbacks.
rubric: Names the authority and every projection's scope.
Covers deletes, corrections, duplicates, and out-of-order updates.
Defines measurable lag and reconciliation.
Provides a no-downtime rebuild and cutover verification.
cases.rationale: Calling a search or graph store a cache hides correctness, rebuild, and authority contracts.

cases.title: Wikitext cost ladder and export honesty
cases.prompt: Use the attached wikitext package to choose among tokenizer events, event trees, parse trees, and incremental sessions. Verify public exports before giving code, explain memory and update costs, and handle the observed missing-export mismatch without inventing an API.
rubric: Chooses the cheapest representation that satisfies the requested operation.
Distinguishes experimental protocols from public exports.
Does not invent the missing parser/session entrypoint.
Uses source inspection and exact-output fixtures for verification.
cases.rationale: The archive contains promising experiments and missing-export evidence, so API confidence must follow the actual entrypoint.

cases.title: Query pagination cannot outrun authorization
cases.prompt: Review a list endpoint that applies tenant authorization after database pagination and accepts arbitrary sort fields. Repair the query contract so filters, authorization, stable ordering, cursor encoding, counts, and complexity limits operate over the same authorized relation.
rubric: Moves authorization into the database relation before counts and pagination.
Rejects arbitrary sort and filter expressions.
Defines stable cursor encoding and invalid-cursor behavior.
Tests cross-tenant and row-deletion cases.
cases.rationale: A syntactically valid query API still leaks or skips data if authorization and pagination do not share one relation.

cases.title: Artifact manifest as a replay contract
cases.prompt: Define a manifest for raw, normalized, enriched, and published pipeline artifacts. Include immutable identity, content digest, schema/version, producer revision, source cursor, parents, row counts, validation status, storage URI, retention, and commit state, then explain how a resumed stage verifies it.
rubric: Separates an attempted artifact from a committed artifact.
Makes provenance and schema compatibility machine-checkable.
Defines retention without deleting required replay inputs.
Verifies bytes and metadata before reuse.
cases.rationale: A filename alone cannot prove artifact identity, completeness, lineage, or safe resumability.

cases.title: Projection deletes and corrections are first-class
cases.prompt: A search projection only handles creates and updates; deleted and corrected source rows remain searchable forever. Add authoritative tombstones or replacement semantics, ordered source positions, idempotent application, lag metrics, reconciliation, and a generation rebuild/cutover.
rubric: Defines deletion and correction semantics from the authority.
Prevents old events from resurrecting stale documents.
Makes lag and drift observable.
Proves rebuild and atomic cutover.
cases.rationale: Projection correctness includes disappearance and correction, not only successful upserts.

cases.title: Classify authority before selecting storage
cases.prompt: Classify user accounts, workflow state, clickstream analytics, full-text search, RDF relationships, and replay artifacts across PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, a search engine, a graph store, and object storage. For every placement name the authority, projection path, consistency, correction, rebuild, and query contract.
rubric: Classifies by workload and authority instead of familiarity.
Explains projection transfer and consistency.
Defines correction and rebuild for every derived store.
Rejects cross-engine feature assumptions.
cases.rationale: Using every store as a peer authority creates irreconcilable drift and cross-engine hallucinations.

cases.title: Checkpoint only committed stage output
cases.prompt: Define a resumable pipeline stage that reads an input manifest, writes output to a temporary location, validates counts/schema/digest, atomically commits a manifest, advances the source cursor, and publishes downstream work. State the transaction or reconciliation boundary for each step.
rubric: Distinguishes attempted input from committed output.
Makes cursor advancement atomic or repairable.
Prevents downstream publication before durable acceptance.
Includes crash injection at every boundary.
cases.rationale: An attempted-item cursor loses or duplicates data when output commitment and publication are separate.

cases.title: Resume validates lineage before skipping work
cases.prompt: A pipeline wants to skip every stage marked successful after code, schema, configuration, and source data changed. Define stage identity, input lineage, producer revision, compatibility rules, invalidation propagation, selective replay, external-effect idempotency, and final reconciliation.
rubric: Skips work only when inputs and producer semantics remain compatible.
Propagates invalidation through the dependency graph.
Protects already-committed external effects.
Reconciles the final published state.
cases.rationale: A success bit without lineage and producer identity turns resume into stale-data reuse.
